target.title: fix(agents): surface MCP structured content in tool results
target.body:
## Summary

- Surface inbound bundle-MCP `structuredContent` in model-visible tool output even when the MCP server also returns normal text content.
- Preserve the existing original content blocks and `details.structuredContent` metadata so UI/log consumers keep the same structured data.
- Keep the change scoped to the bundle-MCP materialization boundary; provider transports, Codex runtime behavior, and MCP server behavior are intentionally out of scope.

## Linked context

Closes #87511

Related #77024
Related #57461

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request; this follows the issue's source-level triage and suggested repair shape.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: MCP servers such as Codex can return `content` plus `structuredContent.threadId`; OpenClaw previously hid the structured fields from the model-facing tool result whenever `content` was non-empty.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node `v22.22.3`, pnpm `11.2.2` via corepack.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local OpenClaw source smoke with `PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:$PATH" node --import tsx --input-type=module -`, importing `materializeBundleMcpToolsForRun`, materializing a Codex-like bundle MCP tool, and executing it against a response containing both `content` and `structuredContent.threadId`.
- Evidence after fix: Copied console output from that local OpenClaw source smoke:

```json
{
  "content": [
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "pong"
    },
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "structuredContent:\n{\n  \"threadId\": \"019e6cdb-8e7f-7cb2-891f-9edb689f6fc7\",\n  \"content\": \"pong\"\n}"
    }
  ],
  "details": {
    "mcpServer": "codex",
    "mcpTool": "codex",
    "structuredContent": {
      "threadId": "019e6cdb-8e7f-7cb2-891f-9edb689f6fc7",
      "content": "pong"
    }
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The model-visible tool result now includes both the original `pong` text block and a second `structuredContent:` JSON text block containing the Codex-style `threadId`, while `details.structuredContent` remains preserved for internal consumers.
- What was not tested: A live OpenClaw session against `codex mcp-server` and a real `codex__codex-reply` continuation.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The after-fix proof uses the real OpenClaw bundle-MCP materialization boundary in a local source checkout, but it does not launch an external Codex MCP server because this environment is not configured with Codex MCP credentials.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Current source only serialized `structuredContent` in the empty-content fallback path, so the same Codex-style response previously exposed only the `pong` text block to the model.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:$PATH" corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:$PATH" node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts`
- `PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:$PATH" corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-materialize.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added focused bundle-MCP materialization coverage for a tool response that includes both normal text content and structuredContent with a Codex-style `threadId`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The model-visible result only included the normal text content, so continuation identifiers stored in `structuredContent` were unavailable to the agent.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Agents can now see serialized structured MCP result fields in tool output.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Prompt-size growth for MCP tools that already return large text content plus large structuredContent.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change is limited to the existing bundle-MCP result boundary and only appends structuredContent when a server explicitly returns it. It preserves existing content and details behavior instead of changing MCP execution or provider transport paths.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer/ClawSweeper review, especially on whether the serialized block label and append behavior are the preferred shape.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

A live Codex MCP smoke is still not provided from this environment.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Updated the PR body with copied local console output for the real behavior proof gate.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the touched code path and ran the focused validation commands above.

target.title: fix(agents): concatenate signature_delta chunks in transport stream
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the root cause of permanent session corruption when using Anthropic models with extended thinking enabled.

The `anthropic-transport-stream.ts` (used by the embedded agent runner) was **overwriting** `thinkingSignature` on each `signature_delta` event instead of **appending**. Since Anthropic sends the thinking block signature across multiple streaming chunks, only the last chunk survived. The truncated signature was persisted to session JSONL, and all subsequent replay attempts failed with HTTP 400.

## Root Cause

```typescript
// BEFORE (bug): overwrites on each delta chunk
block.thinkingSignature = delta.signature;

// AFTER (fix): concatenates all delta chunks
block.thinkingSignature = (block.thinkingSignature || "") + delta.signature;
```

The correct implementation already existed in the same repo at `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts:629-634` — this fix aligns the transport stream with that reference.

## Changes

- **`src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts`** — One-line fix: append signature deltas instead of overwriting
- **`src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts`** — Updated 2 existing assertions + added dedicated multi-chunk concatenation test
- **`test/proof/thinking-signature-real-proof.ts`** — Real-scenario proof exercising the patched code path + live API replay validation

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: When OpenClaw stores assistant messages with Anthropic thinking blocks, the `signature` field is truncated (only last `signature_delta` chunk kept). On replay, Anthropic rejects the invalid signature with HTTP 400, permanently bricking the session. Fixes #87574.

- **Real environment tested**: macOS arm64, OpenClaw 0.6.0-dev gateway, Claude Sonnet 4 via Anthropic API proxy, Node.js v22.22.1. The patched code path section exercises `createAnthropicMessagesTransportStreamFn` directly with crafted multi-chunk SSE events. The live API section uses real Anthropic streaming with `thinking.type: "enabled"`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
cd /tmp/openclaw-src
git checkout fix/thinking-block-storage
export OPENAI_API_KEY="<api-key>"
export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL="<anthropic-compatible-endpoint>"
node --import tsx test/proof/thinking-signature-real-proof.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Terminal output from the real proof script:

```
=== Patched Code Path Proof: createAnthropicMessagesTransportStreamFn ===

  This section exercises the ACTUAL patched transport stream function
  with a mocked fetch serving crafted SSE events.

  Calling createAnthropicMessagesTransportStreamFn()...
  SSE payload: 5 signature_delta events

  ✅ PASS: Transport stream produced a thinking block
  ✅ PASS: Transport stream produced a text block
  ✅ PASS: Thinking text captured correctly (55 chars)
  ✅ PASS: Signature equals concatenation of all 5 chunks (75 chars)
  ✅ PASS: Signature is NOT just the last chunk (old overwrite bug would produce "rX4uVnB9oI6mKpL")
  ✅ PASS: Signature length 75 === sum of chunk lengths 75
  ✅ PASS: All signature chunks appear in order (verifies append, not prepend/shuffle)
  ✅ PASS: Text block content preserved correctly

--- Patched Code Path: 8 passed, 0 failed ---

=== Live API Replay Proof: Thinking Block Signature Integrity ===

API Base: <anthropic-compatible-endpoint>
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6

Step 1: Sending request with extended thinking enabled...
Step 2: Parsing SSE stream...

Step 3: Validation Results

--- Event Summary ---
  message_start: 1
  content_block_start: 2
  content_block_delta: 1766
  content_block_stop: 2
  message_delta: 1
  message_stop: 1

--- Signature Delta Analysis ---
  Total signature_delta events: 1
  Chunk 1: 432 chars "Er8CCl0IDhABGAIqQBXVqq4oYahHsQ..."

--- Signature Comparison ---
  Full signature (concatenated): 432 chars
  Overwrite-only (OLD BUG):      432 chars
  Thinking text length:          127 chars

--- Assertions ---
  ✅ PASS: At least one signature_delta event received
  ℹ️  INFO: Only 1 signature_delta chunk received (proxy/Bedrock may coalesce chunks)
         Multi-chunk concatenation verified by patched code path section above.
  ✅ PASS: Signature captured successfully (432 chars)
  ✅ PASS: Thinking text was captured from thinking_delta events

  ℹ️  Single-chunk delivery — simulating truncation for negative proof

--- Replay Validation ---
  Sending replay request with the captured signature...
  ✅ PASS: Replay with CORRECT (concatenated) signature succeeds: HTTP 200
  Sending replay with TRUNCATED (old bug) signature...
  ✅ PASS: Replay with TRUNCATED (old bug) signature fails: HTTP 400 — confirms the bug causes API rejection

--- Live API Replay: 5 passed, 0 failed ---

=== OVERALL PROOF SUMMARY ===
  Patched code path: 8 passed, 0 failed
  Live API replay:   5 passed, 0 failed
  Total:             13 passed, 0 failed

✅ PROOF PASSED — signature concatenation fix verified
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The proof demonstrates three critical behaviors:
  1. **Patched code path proof**: The actual `createAnthropicMessagesTransportStreamFn` function is called with 5 crafted `signature_delta` SSE events. The output `thinkingSignature` equals the concatenation of all 5 chunks (75 chars), not just the last chunk (15 chars). This would FAIL without the patch (old behavior would produce only "rX4uVnB9oI6mKpL").
  2. **Positive replay proof**: A thinking block signature captured from real Anthropic streaming replays successfully (HTTP 200).
  3. **Negative replay proof**: A truncated signature (simulating the old overwrite bug) is rejected by Anthropic with HTTP 400 — confirming this is the exact mechanism that permanently bricked sessions.

- **What was not tested**: Multi-chunk `signature_delta` delivery was not observed from the live API endpoint (AWS Bedrock proxy coalesces chunks). Multi-chunk concatenation through the actual patched function is verified by the patched code path section which feeds 5 separate `signature_delta` events directly into `createAnthropicMessagesTransportStreamFn`.

## Related Issues

- Fixes #87574
- Refs #80625 — Permanent session corruption from thinking blocks (this is the root cause)
- Refs #85781 — Claude signed thinking blocks poisoning session replay (P1)
- Refs #87475 — Invalid signature errors reaching users
- Refs #87329 — Subagent echo messages amplifying thinking block errors

target.title: Bound aggregate persisted tool results
target.body:
## Summary
- bound the aggregate persisted `toolResult` text kept on the active session branch
- preserve the existing per-result cap while adding a larger aggregate cap for repeated sub-cap outputs
- cover long-running tools that emit many individually acceptable results but can poison later prompts

## Why
A long-running tool can append many `toolResult` messages where each result is under the per-message cap. The persisted session then replays the aggregate output on later turns, so even a short follow-up can exceed the model context window.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** User-reported OpenClaw 2026.5.26 failure with concrete runtime/error evidence: on a real Telegram/Windows setup, a long-running `process` task persisted many individually sub-cap `toolResult` chunks. Later one-character Telegram replies such as `1` failed with `Your input exceeds the context window of this model` because the direct session history was already poisoned by aggregate tool output.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows/Telegram OpenClaw 2026.5.26 bot session (`agent:main:telegram:default:direct:8589344021`) plus this branch in a local OpenClaw checkout on macOS.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Reproduced the same shape with repeated `process`-named tool results against the real `SessionManager`/`installSessionToolResultGuard` path in this branch, then inspected the active branch aggregate after each append. Also verified the original real session backup contained many `toolName: process` `toolResult` entries around ~30k chars each before reset.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the patched guard path showing aggregate persisted tool output stays bounded instead of accumulating unbounded across repeated process results:

  ```text
  scenario: 8 process toolResult messages, each ~850 chars, maxToolResultChars=1000
  previous aggregate shape: 8 * ~853 chars = ~6824 chars persisted on the active branch
  patched active branch result:
  {
    "toolResultMessages": 8,
    "aggregateToolResultChars": 3998,
    "aggregateBudgetChars": 4000,
    "hasTruncationNotice": true,
    "activeBranchMessages": 16
  }
  real-session failure evidence before fix/reset from the 2026.5.26 report: Telegram replies such as "1" returned "Your input exceeds the context window of this model"; inspection of the persisted direct session showed repeated large `toolName: process` / `toolResult` chunks, many around ~30k chars, accumulated before the session was reset.
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Repeated sub-cap tool outputs no longer grow the active branch's persisted `toolResult` text without bound; once aggregate output exceeds the configured aggregate budget, older persisted branch entries are rewritten with truncation notices before the next user turn can replay the full accumulated output.
- **What was not tested:** I did not hotpatch the user's production Windows `dist` runtime with this unmerged PR; the user's preference was to avoid further Windows hotfixes unless official packages are unusable.

## Test plan
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-result-truncation.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-result-truncation.ts`

Note: `pnpm check:changed` reached the core tsgo typecheck stage but was killed by the local runner before completion.

commands.author: klioen

target.title: fix: prevent toolDiscovery from overwriting pinned channel registry
target.body:
When tools.catalog is called from the frontend, it triggers ensureStandalonePluginToolRegistryLoaded with surface=channel and toolDiscovery=true. In toolDiscovery mode, the loaded registry contains no channels, but installStandaloneRegistry still overwrites the pinned channel registry with this empty registry, causing 'Outbound not configured for channel: feishu' errors.

Add a guard: if toolDiscovery is enabled and the registry has no channels, return early before the surface switch that would overwrite the channel registry.

## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- After gateway restart, any frontend page load calls `tools.catalog`, which triggers `ensureStandalonePluginToolRegistryLoaded(surface="channel", toolDiscovery=true)`. In toolDiscovery mode the loaded registry has zero channels, but `installStandaloneRegistry` still calls `pinActivePluginChannelRegistry` with that empty registry, overwriting the pinned channel registry that was correctly set during gateway startup. This causes all outbound channel operations (feishu, discord, slack, etc.) to fail with "Outbound not configured for channel: <id>".

Why does this matter now?

- This is a reliability regression: every time a user opens the frontend UI after a gateway restart, the channel registry is silently wiped. Any cron jobs, outbound messages, or channel-dependent features break until the next gateway restart — and even then, opening the frontend again re-triggers the wipe.

What is the intended outcome?

- When `toolDiscovery=true` and the loaded registry has no channels, `installStandaloneRegistry` returns early after `setActivePluginRegistry` but before the `switch (surface)` block that would overwrite the pinned channel registry. The active registry is still updated (preserving tool discovery behavior), but the channel registry pin is left intact.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Changes to `ensureStandalonePluginToolRegistryLoaded`'s hardcoded `surface: "channel"` parameter.
- Changes to the cache key logic that causes toolDiscovery and full-mode loads to produce different cache keys.
- Defensive fallbacks in `getActivePluginChannelRegistry` or the outbound delivery path.
- The `params.manifestRecord.manifest.id` vs `params.manifestRecord.id` inconsistency in `loader.ts` (separate issue).

What does success look like?

- After gateway restart, opening the frontend and calling `tools.catalog` no longer wipes the channel registry. Outbound channel operations (feishu, etc.) continue to work without requiring a second restart.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the guard condition `toolDiscovery && registry.channels.length === 0` is the correct and complete check, or if there are other scenarios where a channel-less registry should still be allowed to overwrite the pin.
- Whether returning after `setActivePluginRegistry` but before the `switch (surface)` block could break any downstream consumers that expect the channel pin to be updated even in toolDiscovery mode.

<details>
<summary>Summary guidance</summary>

This PR description is the contributor's durable explanation of the change. Write it for human maintainers first; ClawSweeper and Barnacle use the same text to understand intent, proof, risk, and current review state.

Describe the intent and outcome in 2-5 bullets. Avoid restating the diff; reviewers and bots can read the changed files.

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

</details>

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No — discovered through live debugging of a production incident.

<details>
<summary>Linked context guidance</summary>

Link the issue, PR, discussion, maintainer request, or owner request that explains why this PR should exist. Maintainer context helps reviewers and automation distinguish intended work from drive-by churn.

</details>

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: The channel registry was incorrectly pinned when tool discovery ran, which prevented inbound channel messaging.

**Environment tested**: Local OpenClaw setup running on macOS.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. Added regression test targeting the specific failure.
2. Run command `pnpm test src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
<details>
<summary>Terminal Output</summary>

```text
$ pnpm test src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/bytedance/Code/openclaw

 ✓ |plugins| src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts (3 tests) 15ms
     ✓ reuses a compatible gateway startup registry for gateway-bindable dispatch load options 10ms
     ✓ loads a fresh registry when dispatch config is not startup-compatible 2ms
     ✓ does not pin a tool-discovery registry when channels are empty 3ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)
   Start at  20:52:39
   Duration  1.29s (transform 743ms, setup 124ms, import 1.06s, tests 15ms, environment 0ms)
```
</details>

**Observed result after fix**: The tests pass. The active channel registry correctly returns `null` instead of being overwritten and pinned by an empty tool-discovery registry.

**What was not tested**: Full end-to-end multi-channel deployment behavior is not included in this terminal snippet.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm build` — verified the patch compiles without errors
- Manual integration test on live deployment with diagnostic logging

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- None — this is a guard against a runtime state corruption that is difficult to reproduce in unit tests without mocking the entire plugin loading pipeline

What failed before this fix, if known?

- All outbound channel operations (feishu message delivery, cron.run) failed with "Outbound not configured for channel: feishu" after frontend page load triggered `tools.catalog`

If no test was added, why not?

- The bug requires a full gateway runtime with plugin loading, channel binding, and a frontend WebSocket call to reproduce. The existing test infrastructure for `standalone-runtime-registry-loader` does not cover the interaction between `toolDiscovery` mode and `pinActivePluginChannelRegistry`. Adding a meaningful unit test would require significant mocking of the plugin loading pipeline, which may not provide reliable regression coverage.

<details>
<summary>Testing guidance</summary>

List focused commands, not every incidental check. CI is useful support, but external PRs still need real behavior proof above when behavior changes.

</details>

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No — the fix prevents silent state corruption; no user-facing API or output changes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

What is the highest-risk area?

- The guard could theoretically prevent a legitimate channel registry update in toolDiscovery mode. However, toolDiscovery mode by design does not load channel surfaces (`runtimeChannel=false`), so a registry with `channels.length === 0` in toolDiscovery mode is expected behavior, not a missing update.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The guard only applies when both conditions are true: `toolDiscovery=true` AND `registry.channels.length === 0`. In full mode (`toolDiscovery=false`), the channel registry is still updated normally. In toolDiscovery mode with a non-empty channels array (which should not happen but is theoretically possible), the registry would also be updated normally.

<details>
<summary>Risk guidance</summary>

Use this for author judgment that is not obvious from the diff. ClawSweeper can see touched files, but it cannot know which behavior you think is risky, why the risk is acceptable, or what mitigation reviewers should verify.

</details>

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Awaiting maintainer review

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI checks need to pass; maintainer review needed

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- N/A — first submission

<details>
<summary>Review state guidance</summary>

Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Real behavior proof (Test output)</summary>

```text
$ pnpm test src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts

 ✓ |plugins| src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts (3 tests) 14ms

target.title: Fix Discord progress final and status command output
target.body:
## Summary

- omit `command-output` titles when channel progress is configured with `commandText: "status"`
- route Discord `onCommandOutput` progress through the same config-aware formatter as tool/item progress
- keep raw/default behavior unchanged so command-output titles still render outside status mode
- keep Discord `progress` previews temporary by clearing the live progress draft before sending the final reply normally
- clear/cancel pending and in-flight progress drafts even before Discord has assigned a draft message id
- suppress late Discord error finals after a visible final reply has already been queued/delivered, so a trailing tool warning cannot replace or follow the real answer
- add regression assertions for the shared formatter and Discord progress/final delivery behavior

## Why

`commandText: "status"` already suppresses command text for command/tool progress, but `command-output` lines still appended `input.title`. That can expose internal command titles in channel progress even when the channel is configured for status-only command display.

The shared formatter handled this correctly, but Discord's `onCommandOutput` callback was still calling the raw formatter directly. This branch now passes Discord command-output events through the config-aware formatter, matching the existing `onToolStart` and `onItemEvent` behavior.

A second Discord-specific failure remained after that formatter fix: in `progress` streaming mode the live tool-progress draft could be treated as the final reply container. If the turn hit failed tools, compaction, or fresh-thread pressure, Discord could leave the progress bubble visible as the last apparent assistant message instead of showing the final assistant reply. Progress previews should stay temporary; final replies should go through the normal message path.

There is also a pending-draft race: a final reply can arrive while the progress draft send is still pending or in flight and no Discord message id exists yet. Final cleanup must call the draft cleanup path anyway, because that is what cancels pending work and waits for in-flight sends before the normal final reply is delivered.

Finally, after the assistant has already queued/delivered a visible final reply, a subsequent tool-error final can still be queued as a second final. In Discord this can make the final visible event look like the tool failure instead of the answer. Once a non-error final reply is accepted, later error finals are suppressed; error finals still deliver when they are the only final outcome.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: A `command-output` progress event with `commandText: "status"` should render the status/exit code without appending the command-output title. Discord `progress` drafts should be cleared before the final reply is delivered normally, including the case where no draft message id exists yet, and late tool-error finals should not appear after a visible final reply.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch, plus redacted live Discord gateway smoke runs using the built Discord extension from this branch. The final smoke used an isolated Discord gateway configured with `streaming.mode: "progress"`, `progress.toolProgress: true`, and `progress.commandText: "status"`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

1. Render a failed `command-output` event with default options and with `{ commandText: "status" }`.
2. Run the Discord message-handler regression suite covering config-aware command-output progress, progress draft final delivery, pending/no-id progress draft cleanup, and late error-final suppression.
3. Build/load the Discord extension from this PR into an isolated Discord gateway.
4. Configure the isolated gateway with `progress.commandText: "status"` and restart only that isolated gateway.
5. Send a live Discord prompt that forces one tool command equivalent to `printf '<redacted_marker>\n'; exit 42`, then returns a normal final assistant message.

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node --import tsx - <<'JS'
import { formatChannelProgressDraftLine } from './src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.ts';
const input = {
  event: 'command-output',
  name: 'exec',
  exitCode: 1,
  title: 'run internal config check failed',
};
console.log('default:', formatChannelProgressDraftLine(input));
console.log('status:', formatChannelProgressDraftLine(input, { commandText: 'status' }));
JS
default: 🛠️ exit 1; run internal config check failed
status: 🛠️ exit 1
```

```console
$ pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  77 passed (77)
```

```console
$ pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  22 passed (22)
```

```text
Live Discord smoke, isolated gateway with commandText=status:
- the PR-built Discord extension was selected by explicit plugin config
- progress mode was active with tool progress enabled and command text set to status
- the assistant executed the requested tool command and the runtime recorded exitCode=42 with the redacted stdout marker
- Discord message history after the turn contained the normal final assistant reply marker as the latest assistant message
- no `exit 42` tool-error final replaced or followed the normal assistant final
```

Observed result after fix: Default/raw behavior still includes the title. Status mode renders only `exit 1`, so the command-output title is suppressed. Discord progress mode now uses that config-aware formatter for real `onCommandOutput` callbacks; the Discord regression asserts the progress draft contains `exit 1` and not the command-output title. Discord progress-mode final replies no longer edit the progress draft in place; tests assert the progress draft is cleared, pending/no-id progress drafts still call `clear()` before final delivery, `deliverDiscordReply` sends the final separately, and later tool-warning finals are suppressed after a visible final. The live Discord status-mode smoke matched the delivery behavior: a failed tool with exit code 42 was followed by the normal assistant final as the visible final message.

What was not tested: A Telegram transport smoke was not run for this PR. The shared formatter behavior is covered by plugin-sdk tests; the live transport proof above is Discord-specific.

## Test

- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts`

target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Harden Codex dynamic-tool registration so malformed runtime tool schemas are diagnosed and quarantined instead of failing assistant turn startup.
- Validate nested JSON Schema shape hazards that Codex rejects, including malformed schema maps, schema arrays, optional schema-valued keywords, `required`, property dependencies, and dynamic schema keywords.
- Preserve valid current-turn executable tools when only stale registered metadata for the same name is invalid.
- Inline local `$ref` schemas under MCP/tool-schema dependency and additional-item keywords so `$defs` are not stripped while dangling refs remain.
- Keep MCP loopback `tools/list` alive when a tool schema union contains malformed property-schema variants; malformed variants are ignored while later valid variants can still define the property.
- Preserve optionality when MCP loopback union schemas include boolean `true` branches instead of over-tightening merged `required` fields.
- Prefer later usable MCP union property schemas over earlier boolean `false` property variants so `tools/list` does not forbid valid arguments.
- Clean Gemini provider compatibility schemas inside schema-valued dependency and conditional branches so inspection and normalization agree.
- Clean shared provider compatibility schemas inside schema-valued dependency, conditional, tuple, and additional-item branches so xAI-style inspection and normalization agree.
- Make plugin tool descriptor cache keys stable and resilient to non-JSON-safe ambient tool context metadata.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible `defineToolPlugin` config and tool parameter schemas at the SDK boundary before they can enter metadata/runtime registration.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible plugin factory tool parameter schemas before they enter shared runtime tool lists or the descriptor cache.
- Validate tool-plugin authoring metadata read from the SDK symbol so forged entries cannot feed cyclic/BigInt schemas into manifest generation.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible plugin config schema validator inputs before recursive schema walking, cache fingerprinting, or TypeBox compilation.
- Reject unreadable plugin config values before schema validation/default cloning so hostile config payloads become structured validation errors instead of raw proxy or `structuredClone` crashes.
- Bound Plugin SDK provider catalog projection so unreadable catalog maps or hostile model array methods cannot crash unified model catalog row generation.
- Copy Plugin SDK provider auth/env arrays before registration so hostile source array methods cannot crash provider registration.
- Copy provider setup wizard auth arrays before option, model-picker, and choice resolution so hostile source array methods cannot crash setup flows.
- Bound paired provider catalog map/config reads so hostile provider catalog metadata cannot crash API-key catalog generation.
- Copy SDK channel allowlists before coercion so hostile `allowFrom` array methods cannot crash channel config helpers.
- Copy SDK access-group allowlists before parsing/expansion so hostile `allowFrom` array methods cannot crash command/DM authorization helpers.
- Copy SDK access-group sender member lists before matching so hostile member iterators cannot crash command/DM authorization helpers.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible bundle-MCP draft-2020-12 schemas before recursive schema walking or TypeBox compilation.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible plugin config schema metadata before recursive normalization or metadata exposure.
- Omit non-JSON-compatible extension config schemas from merged config schema responses before clone/merge paths can throw.
- Sanitize circular, unreadable, and non-JSON-compatible Responses input payload trees before OpenAI/Azure request serialization.
- Sanitize Codex trajectory payloads so non-JSON-compatible synthetic tool metadata cannot crash trajectory recording or bypass redaction.
- Reject unreadable plugin host-hook JSON payloads without throwing from host validation.
- Ignore unreadable Codex MCP elicitation request/display fields without throwing out of approval bridging.
- Sanitize unreadable Codex preserved-context tool payloads so context-engine projection cannot crash on hostile synthetic payloads.
- Fail closed on unreadable Codex app-server approval routing, permission-summary, policy-rewrite, and command payloads.
- Tolerate unreadable Codex app-server protocol response/turn fields before schema validation.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server notification correlation fields before routing/logging decisions.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server projector hook routing fields before event projection.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server side-question notification/request fields before side-thread routing.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server prompt-report schema and notification fields before report hashing or runtime routing decisions.
- Omit unreadable Codex diagnostic tool parameter schemas before model-call diagnostic capture.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server user-input request and resolution fields before chat prompt routing.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server attempt notification-state params before watchdog and terminal-state decisions.
- Ignore unreadable Codex native subagent notification params/item/content fields before completion extraction.
- Ignore unreadable Codex native subagent task-mirror params/thread/status/item fields before task lifecycle mirroring.
- Ignore unreadable Codex native subagent monitor params/thread/spawn/item fields before child-thread and completion routing.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server JSON-RPC message params/result fields before request, notification, or pending-response dispatch.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server tool-progress and mirrored-history sanitizer fields before logging or image-payload sanitation.
- Redact Codex app inventory error data through catch-bounded reads so unreadable synthetic fields cannot replace the original refresh failure.
- Fingerprint unreadable, circular, and non-JSON-compatible Codex dynamic tool fields without crashing thread lifecycle decisions.
- Fail closed on unreadable Codex plugin detail app fields before plugin app exposure reaches thread config.
- Fail closed on unreadable Codex plugin list marketplace fields before plugin app exposure reaches thread config.
- Fail closed on unreadable Codex plugin activation list/state fields before migration can attempt `plugin/install`.
- Normalize unreadable Codex app inventory entries before ownership and app exposure decisions.
- Normalize unreadable Codex plugin install auth-app summaries before activation diagnostics.
- Fail closed on unreadable Codex Computer Use marketplace discovery before install attempts.
- Fail closed on unreadable Codex Computer Use MCP status tool fields after install.
- Fail closed on unreadable Codex Computer Use plugin detail before install attempts.
- Normalize unreadable Codex Computer Use plugin summaries before install-state decisions.
- Ignore unreadable Codex Computer Use marketplace-add names before marketplace discovery.
- Normalize unreadable Codex Computer Use MCP tool maps before tool-name extraction.
- Copy Codex Computer Use response arrays before marketplace/MCP traversal so hostile array methods cannot crash setup.
- Copy provider compatibility tool/schema arrays before Gemini/DeepSeek/OpenAI traversal so hostile array methods or iterators cannot crash schema normalization.
- Omit unreadable Gemini/DeepSeek schema arrays instead of leaking hostile proxies or emitting empty unions.
- Bound provider compatibility schema object enumeration before Gemini/DeepSeek/OpenAI/xAI traversal so hostile object key traps cannot crash schema normalization.
- Reuse one shared JSON-compatibility walker across SDK and runtime plugin boundaries.
- Report unreadable fields from the shared JSON-compatibility walker as deterministic validation errors instead of leaking getter/enumeration exceptions.
- Keep exec-tool failure logging bounded when command/env params have unreadable synthetic fields, preserving the original tool error and redaction policy.
- Report unreadable tool-result `content` / `details` fields as stable adapter errors instead of leaking getter exceptions from synthetic plugin results.
- Keep plugin lifecycle trace logging from replacing original lifecycle failures when synthetic trace details have unreadable fields.
- Tolerate unreadable plugin descriptor-cache context metadata while preserving deterministic cache keys for readable fields.
- Tolerate cyclic and BigInt provider runtime config metadata while preserving type-tagged cache keys that do not alias ordinary strings.
- Use a shared type-tagged plugin cache-key encoder for loader/provider cache inputs so non-JSON-safe plugin config cannot crash or alias cache entries.
- Rename legacy schema-warning test fixtures to synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `fuzz_move_*` names so the PR no longer keeps the reported third-party plugin name in current test content.
- Keep test fixtures synthetic (`fuzzplugin`/`fuzz_move_*`) instead of naming the reported third-party plugin.
- Move plugin tool metadata storage into a lightweight module so schema diagnostics can name plugin/tool ownership without importing heavier plugin runtime code.
- Out of scope: changing global plugin materialization policy or rejecting valid non-Codex schemas outside the Codex/doctor projection path.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Codex dynamic-tool registration could reject malformed active tool schemas before content was produced.
- Shared SDK/runtime JSON compatibility validation could throw getter/enumeration exceptions instead of returning deterministic non-JSON-compatible errors.
- Plugin config validation could throw unreadable-value getter errors or `structuredClone` failures before returning a normal validation result.
- Plugin SDK provider catalog projection could throw unreadable catalog-map getter errors before returning unified model rows.
- Plugin SDK provider registration could throw from plugin-provided provider auth array methods before registering an otherwise valid provider.
- Provider setup wizard resolution could throw from plugin-provided provider auth array methods before rendering setup choices or resolving selected provider auth methods.
- Paired provider catalog generation could throw from plugin-provided provider map enumeration or provider config spreading before returning a catalog result.
- Plugin SDK channel allowlist normalization could throw from plugin-provided `allowFrom` array methods before returning channel config helper results.
- Plugin SDK access-group allowlist expansion could throw from plugin-provided `allowFrom` array methods before returning command/DM authorization helper results.
- Plugin SDK access-group sender matching could throw from plugin-provided member list iterators before returning command/DM authorization helper results.
- Exec-tool failure logging could let unreadable param getters replace the original tool error and risk leaking getter text instead of preserving redacted failure logs.
- Broad changed-gate proof exposed current-main test-only type errors in malformed policy/webhook fixtures; those are narrowed in this branch so the gate can complete.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed Codex dynamic tools are now disabled instead of being advertised or crashing registration.

What is the highest-risk area?

Overly strict schema-shape validation could disable a valid runtime tool schema.

How is that risk mitigated?

The projection preserves valid JSON Schema forms covered by review, including boolean schemas, tuple `items`, `dependencies` string arrays, and schema-valued `additionalItems`; focused tests and changed-gate proof cover the touched surfaces.

target.title: feat(agents): inherit requester model for subagents
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `model: "inherit"` support for native sub-agent spawns so child runs can follow the requester session's active model.
- Threads the requester model context from tool creation through `sessions_spawn` into direct sub-agent planning, with a persisted-session fallback when needed.
- Documents the new sub-agent model inheritance behavior and config precedence.
- Out of scope: ACP runtime model inheritance; ACP spawns keep their existing model handling.

Review focus: model-selection precedence, the missing-requester-model error path, and whether docs describe the new behavior clearly.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No formal maintainer request; this addresses a configuration gap where sessions can use different active models but derived sub-agents could not intentionally follow the requester model.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: native `sessions_spawn` can now use `model: "inherit"` or sub-agent model config set to `"inherit"` to resolve the child model from the requester session.
- Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw checkout at PR head `9a8d70a`, OpenClaw `2026.5.28`; local loopback Gateway started with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`; real provider-backed run on `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` using an OAuth auth profile.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --compact --cli-backend-logs

node scripts/run-node.mjs agent \
  --agent main \
  --session-key agent:main:pr-inherit-proof-20260528-retry-001 \
  --model openai-codex/gpt-5.5 \
  --timeout 900 \
  --json \
  --message 'PR verification run. Do not send any external channel message and do not use the message tool. Use sessions_spawn exactly once with taskName="inherit_model_probe", label="inherit-model-probe", model="inherit", context="isolated", cleanup="keep", runTimeoutSeconds=180, and task="Return exactly CHILD_MODEL_INHERIT_PROBE_OK, then include one short line naming the model/provider you are running under if visible." After spawning, call sessions_yield so the child completion can return to this requester session. When the child result arrives, reply with a concise proof summary including the child taskName, the child result text, and the requester model openai-codex/gpt-5.5.'

node scripts/run-node.mjs sessions --all-agents --active 120 --json
```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture excerpts, redacted to omit local absolute paths):

```json
{
  "runId": "a31fa843-6e0a-4696-aaa2-e3ccc9ed0f94",
  "status": "ok",
  "summary": "completed",
  "result": {
    "meta": {
      "agentMeta": {
        "provider": "openai-codex",
        "model": "gpt-5.5",
        "agentHarnessId": "codex"
      },
      "executionTrace": {
        "winnerProvider": "openai-codex",
        "winnerModel": "gpt-5.5",
        "fallbackUsed": false
      },
      "toolSummary": {
        "calls": 2,
        "tools": ["sessions_spawn", "sessions_yield"],
        "failures": 0
      }
    }
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "parentToolCall": {
    "name": "sessions_spawn",
    "arguments": {
      "taskName": "inherit_model_probe",
      "label": "inherit-model-probe",
      "model": "inherit",
      "context": "isolated",
      "cleanup": "keep",
      "runTimeoutSeconds": 180
    }
  },
  "spawnResult": {
    "status": "accepted",
    "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:5b710938-44c0-4927-a48e-ac1c0e124189",
    "taskName": "inherit_model_probe",
    "modelApplied": true
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "key": "agent:main:subagent:5b710938-44c0-4927-a48e-ac1c0e124189",
  "kind": "spawn-child",
  "model": "gpt-5.5",
  "modelProvider": "openai-codex",
  "providerOverride": "openai-codex",
  "modelOverride": "gpt-5.5",
  "agentRuntime": {
    "id": "codex",
    "source": "implicit"
  },
  "status": "done"
}
```

```json
{
  "childAssistantMessage": {
    "role": "assistant",
    "provider": "openai-codex",
    "model": "gpt-5.5",
    "content": "CHILD_MODEL_INHERIT_PROBE_OK\nModel/provider visible: Codex based on GPT-5."
  },
  "parentFinalMessage": "Proof summary:\n- Child taskName: `inherit_model_probe`\n- Child result: `CHILD_MODEL_INHERIT_PROBE_OK` / `Model/provider visible: Codex based on GPT-5.`\n- Requester model: `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the parent session ran on `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, invoked `sessions_spawn` with `model: "inherit"`, the spawn was accepted with `modelApplied: true`, and the child `spawn-child` session persisted and executed with `modelProvider: "openai-codex"` and `model: "gpt-5.5"`.
- What was not tested: external channel delivery was intentionally disabled with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`; ACP runtime inheritance remains out of scope for this PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof used a local loopback Gateway and a real model provider run, with channel connectors skipped to avoid external delivery.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing tests covered concrete model overrides and config defaults but had no requester-model inheritance path.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/gateway/config-agents.md docs/tools/subagents.md src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn-plan.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.runtime.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test-helpers.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts src/agents/tool-description-presets.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts src/config/types.agents.ts`
- `node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-changed.mjs` (partial; stopped during `tsgo:core:test` after 969s without output)
- `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --compact --cli-backend-logs`
- `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:pr-inherit-proof-20260528-retry-001 --model openai-codex/gpt-5.5 --timeout 900 --json --message '<sessions_spawn model="inherit" proof prompt>'`
- `node scripts/run-node.mjs sessions --all-agents --active 120 --json`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Model-selection tests for explicit `inherit`, configured `inherit`, alias preservation, and missing requester model behavior.
- `sessions_spawn` tool tests to verify active model provider/id is passed into native sub-agent context.
- Direct sub-agent spawn tests to verify requester model context is persisted on the child session.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Sub-agent spawns could select explicit or configured concrete models, but there was no supported way to make a child follow the requester session's active model.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `agents.defaults.subagents.model` and `agents.list[].subagents.model` now accept `"inherit"` as a special value.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Model resolution precedence for native sub-agent spawns.

How is that risk mitigated?

The implementation keeps existing concrete model precedence, resolves `"inherit"` only for sub-agent model candidates, errors when inheritance is requested without a requester model, and adds focused tests at the model-selection, tool, direct-spawn, and live Gateway seams.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review after the real behavior proof check re-runs against the updated PR body.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

The PR body now includes live Gateway proof. Waiting on the automated real behavior proof check to re-run and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed the missing live Gateway proof concern by adding real `sessions_spawn model="inherit"` evidence from PR head.

target.title: docs(plugins): clarify skill workshop tool policy
target.body:
Fixes #87570

## Summary
- clarify that enabling Skill Workshop registers `skill_workshop`, but manual calls still depend on the active tool policy
- document the narrow `tools.alsoAllow: ["skill_workshop"]` fix for `tools.profile: "coding"` users
- update the troubleshooting table so a hidden tool points operators to both plugin enablement and tool-policy checks

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Skill Workshop could be enabled and loaded while manual `skill_workshop` calls remained unavailable under `tools.profile: "coding"`, with the docs saying the tool was available but not pointing to the required tool-policy grant.

Real environment tested: Source docs in this branch, based on `origin/main`, rendered from `docs/plugins/skill-workshop.md`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `nl -ba docs/plugins/skill-workshop.md | sed -n '86,112p;661,668p'`

Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from this branch after the patch:

```text
    87  - the `skill_workshop` tool is registered by the plugin
    92  Manual `skill_workshop` calls still follow the active tool policy. If your
    93  agent uses `tools.profile: "coding"`, add the exact tool to the policy:
    95  ```json5
    97    tools: {
    98      profile: "coding",
    99      alsoAllow: ["skill_workshop"],
   104  Use `alsoAllow: ["group:plugins"]` only when the agent should be allowed to use
   105  all currently loaded plugin-owned tools.
   666 | Tool is unavailable | Plugin entry is not enabled, or the active tool policy hides plugin tools | `plugins.entries.skill-workshop.enabled`, `openclaw plugins list`, and `tools.alsoAllow: ["skill_workshop"]` |
```

Observed result after fix: The Skill Workshop guide now distinguishes plugin registration from tool-policy exposure and gives operators an exact config for manual `status`, `list_pending`, and `apply` calls under `tools.profile: "coding"`.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Gateway or agent session; this is a docs-only clarification for an already source-reproduced tool-policy behavior.

## Verification
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

target.title: feat(ui): render image blocks in tool output cards
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Tool output messages containing `image` content blocks are not rendered in the Control UI tool cards — images in tool results are invisible to the user.
- Solution: Extract image URLs from tool result content blocks (`type: "image"`) and include them in the tool card output, supporting direct URLs, inline base64 data, and structured `{ source: { type: "base64", data, media_type } }` objects.
- What changed: `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts` (image extraction + rendering), `ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.ts` (preserve image blocks), `ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts` (stream handling), `ui/src/styles/chat/tool-cards.css` (styling), `ui/src/ui/types/chat-types.ts` (types).
- What did NOT change: No new plugins, no dependency changes, no lockfile modifications, no config changes. This is a UI-only renderer fix.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Tool output messages with `image` content blocks are invisible in the Control UI tool cards.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node v22, worktree at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87548`. Directly called `extractToolCards()` via `npx tsx` with a tool message containing three image blocks in different source formats.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87548
  npx tsx -e "
  import { extractToolCards } from './ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts';
  const msg = { role: 'tool', content: [
    { type: 'text', text: 'Generated chart:' },
    { type: 'image', url: 'https://example.com/chart.png' },
    { type: 'image', data: 'iVBORw0KGgo=', mimeType: 'image/png' },
    { type: 'image', source: { type: 'base64', data: 'R0lGODlh...', media_type: 'image/gif' } }
  ]};
  console.log(JSON.stringify(extractToolCards(msg)));
  "
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
$ npx tsx proof_ui.mjs

Tool: tool
Images extracted: 3
  https://example.com/chart.png
  data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgo=
  data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7
Output text: Generated chart:...
```
- **Observed result after fix:** All three image source formats are correctly extracted and converted to displayable URLs:
  1. Direct `url` field → kept as-is
  2. Inline `data` + `mimeType` → converted to `data:image/png;base64,...`
  3. Structured `source.data` + `source.media_type` → converted to `data:image/gif;base64,...`
- **What was not tested:** Did not capture a browser screenshot of the rendered Control UI (requires a running gateway with a tool that produces real image output).

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test (existing)
- Target test file: `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: Image content blocks in tool messages are extracted and included in the tool card output array.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The `extractToolImages` function handles all three image source formats used by the OpenClaw protocol (direct URL, inline base64, structured source).

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The tool card builder in `tool-cards.ts` only extracted text from tool output messages, ignoring `image` content blocks entirely.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No image extraction logic existed for tool output content blocks.

target.title: fix(cron): skip deleteAfterRun and preserve counters for manual runs (#83538)
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw cron run` (manual/debug runs) on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes the job on success — consuming the one-shot and preventing its scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters, interfering with scheduled retry/backoff state.

This PR adds an `isManual` flag to `applyJobResult` so manual runs preserve the job and leave scheduled execution state untouched.

## Root cause

`applyJobResult()` has no concept of manual vs scheduled runs. When a manual run succeeds on a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job, `shouldDelete` evaluates to `true` and the job is removed from the store — the scheduled fire never happens.

Additionally, manual error/skipped/success outcomes unconditionally mutate `consecutiveErrors` and `consecutiveSkipped` counters, which control retry backoff and auto-disable behavior for scheduled runs.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `timer.ts` | Add `isManual` option to `applyJobResult`; skip `deleteAfterRun` for manual runs; guard all counter mutations and alert emissions with `!isManual` |
| `ops.ts` | Pass `isManual: true` from manual run completion and invalid-persisted-run handling |
| `timer.regression.test.ts` | 5 new tests: manual ok/error preserve job, manual doesn't increment/reset counters |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts
 ✓ cron (45 tests) 144ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  45 passed (45)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes it, preventing scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate scheduled retry counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `aa92a4a5b0`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.

### Source-level proof

```
Main branch applyJobResult():
  - shouldDelete = kind === "at" && deleteAfterRun && status === "ok"
    → no manual discriminator, always deletes on success
  - consecutiveErrors incremented unconditionally on error
  - consecutiveSkipped incremented unconditionally on skip

Fix branch:
  - shouldDelete = wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual
    → manual runs never trigger deletion
  - Counter mutations guarded: if (!opts?.isManual) { ... }
    → manual runs don't affect retry/backoff state
```

### Regression test coverage

```
✅ manual ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted
✅ manual error on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted  
✅ scheduled ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job deleted (unchanged behavior)
✅ manual error does not increment consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
✅ manual success does not reset consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
```

- **What was not tested**: Live `openclaw cron run` CLI flow (requires a running gateway with a configured `deleteAfterRun` at-job). The `isManual` flag propagation from `ops.ts` to `applyJobResult` is validated at the unit level.

Closes #83538


### Re-verification (2026-05-20)

Local checkout at `d89f14de26`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

 ✓ cron  ../../src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (39 tests) 116ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  39 passed (39)
   Duration  152ms
```

Full cron suite (`src/cron/`) also run: 94 test files, 901 passed. The 7 failures are in unrelated files (`run-log.test.ts`, `service.failure-alert.test.ts`) pre-existing on upstream/main.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `openclaw cron run` (manual) of a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job incorrectly deletes the job. Manual runs also pollute `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `0cec2acc7a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On `upstream/main`, counter mutations and `shouldDelete` run unconditionally — no `isManual` discriminator exists.
- **Exact steps after patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` + `src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 46/46 timer regression tests pass (including 7 new #83538/#83933-specific tests). 7/7 failure-alert tests pass.
- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI flow; the tests exercise the same `applyJobResult` code path the Gateway calls internally.

### Key test results

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun + success | NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun + success | Deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |
| Manual error → failure alert | NOT triggered ✅ |
| Scheduled error → failure alert | Triggered ✅ |


## Real behavior proof (Docker, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker container, `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched source at `885d605e56`.

### Latest fixes

| Issue | Problem | Fix |
|-------|---------|-----|
| P1 | Manual at-job error was disabling job (`enabled=false`, `nextRunAtMs=undefined`) | At-job error retry/disable block now guarded with `if (opts?.isManual)` |
| P2 | Manual success was clearing `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown | Cooldown clearing now inside `if (!opts?.isManual)` guard |

### Test results

| Test Suite | Result |
|------------|--------|
| Timer regression (49 tests) | ✅ All passed |
| Failure-alert (7 tests) | ✅ All passed |

### Key verified behaviors

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual at-job error | `enabled` stays true, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| Manual success | `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown preserved ✅ |
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun | Job NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun | Job deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |


## Real CLI proof (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway at `885d605e56` running with `--allow-unconfigured`.

**Terminal transcript**: `openclaw cron add` → `cron show` → `cron run` (manual, triggers error) → `cron show` (job still exists, enabled=true, nextRunAtMs preserved) → `cron list` (job present) → `cron runs` (run history with error status, nextRunAtMs unchanged).

| Step | Command | Result |
|------|---------|--------|
| Add job | `openclaw cron add --name proof-job --at ... --delete-after-run` | Created with `deleteAfterRun: true` |
| Manual run | `openclaw cron run <id>` | `{"ok": true, "enqueued": true}` |
| After run | `openclaw cron show <id>` | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |
| Job exists | `openclaw cron list` | Job still listed ✅ |
| Run history | `openclaw cron runs --id <id>` | `status: "error"`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |


## Real CLI proof at head SHA `642c3450d9` (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway built from PR head.

| Test | Command | Before (main) | After (PR head) |
|------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| A: at-job deleteAfterRun | `cron run` → `cron show` | Job deleted/disabled | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| B: every-job manual error | `cron run` → `cron show` | `nextRunAtMs` rewritten by backoff | `nextRunAtMs` unchanged ✅ |
| A+B: counters | `cron runs` | `consecutiveErrors` mutated | Unchanged ✅ |

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Cron Jobs list filtering from #9455 now matches job IDs during text search and lets the Jobs last-run status filter select Unknown while still recognizing preferred `lastRunStatus` and legacy `lastStatus` values.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout `/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw-issue-9455` at `849f83bdf9` on Linux, Node `v22.22.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`, running repository code via `node --import tsx` against a temporary cron store JSON.

target.title: fix(cron): guard against undefined sourceDelivery in isolated executor
target.body:
## Summary

Guard against `undefined` `sourceDelivery` in the isolated cron executor to prevent `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')`.

## Problem

Commit `4c613fbfe0` ("refactor(cron): centralize source delivery plan", 2026-05-18) replaced three separate params (`messageChannel`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy`) with a unified `sourceDelivery: SourceDeliveryPlan` across the lazy-import boundary between `run.ts` and `run-executor.ts`.

A runtime version skew (stale compiled JS from a partial build or ESM module cache) causes `params.sourceDelivery` to be `undefined` for isolated cron jobs, crashing at `run-executor.ts:147`.

## Fix

1. Made `sourceDelivery` optional in both `createCronPromptExecutor` and `executeCronRun`
2. When `sourceDelivery` is undefined, reconstruct a `SourceDeliveryPlan` that **preserves legacy caller fields**:
   - Reads `toolPolicy` from stale caller params (`disableMessageTool`, `forceMessageTool`, `requireExplicitMessageTarget`)
   - Reads `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` from stale caller params — selects `owner: "message_tool"` when mode is `"message_tool_only"`, preserving delivery semantics
   - Reads `messageChannel` from stale caller params
   - Falls back to `resolvedDelivery` for channel/to/accountId/threadId
   - Passes `requireExplicitMessageTarget` through to `createSourceDeliveryPlan` (previously omitted, defaulting to `false`)
3. Added `logWarn` diagnostic to flag the fallback

This ensures webhook callers that disabled the message tool still have it disabled, announce callers that set `message_tool_only` keep that mode, `requireExplicitMessageTarget` is honored, and no security boundary is widened.

## Tests

11 test cases in `run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts`:

**`createCronPromptExecutor` level (6 tests):**
1. `sourceDelivery: undefined` (no legacy fields) → reconstructs with safe defaults ✅
2. `sourceDelivery: undefined` → falls back to `resolvedDelivery` channel/to/accountId/threadId ✅
3. Stale announce caller → preserves `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` and tool policy ✅
4. Stale webhook caller → message tool stays disabled ✅
5. `requireExplicitMessageTarget: true` → passed through to plan ✅
6. Valid `sourceDelivery` → works normally (regression) ✅

**`executeCronRun` level (5 tests):**
7. Stale announce caller → `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"` preserved through full execute path ✅
8. No legacy mode → `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` defaults to `undefined` ✅
9. `requireExplicitMessageTarget: true` → passed through executeCronRun fallback ✅
10. Missing toolPolicy → `requireExplicitMessageTarget` defaults to `false` ✅
11. Stale webhook caller → message tool disabled preserved through executeCronRun ✅

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: All `sessionTarget: "isolated"` cron jobs crash with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')`. Additionally, stale announce callers would silently lose `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` and `requireExplicitMessageTarget` through the `executeCronRun` → `createCronPromptExecutor` boundary.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0, source tree at commit `65c7e3812e`

### Failing proof (upstream/main, before fix)

On upstream/main (`13deea2a`), `sourceDelivery` is **required** — no guard exists, no fallback:

```
$ git show 13deea2a9d:src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts | grep -n "sourceDelivery"
112:  sourceDelivery: SourceDeliveryPlan;       # <-- NOT optional
156:  const sourceReplyDeliveryMode = params.sourceDelivery.sourceReplyDeliveryMode;  # <-- direct deref, crashes when undefined
327:  sourceDelivery: SourceDeliveryPlan;       # <-- NOT optional in executeCronRun either
```

The test file does not exist on main:
```
$ git show 13deea2a9d:src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts
fatal: path 'src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts' does not exist in '13deea2a9d'
```

### Passing proof (fix branch, after fix)

**Delivery mode preservation proof** (demonstrates the owner-selection fix):

```
$ node --import tsx scripts/cron-execute-proof.mjs

=== PR #85249 executeCronRun Delivery Mode Proof ===
Environment: darwin arm64 Node v24.10.0

Test 1: Stale announce caller (sourceReplyDeliveryMode: message_tool_only)
  owner resolved to: "message_tool"
  plan.sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"
  plan.messageTool.requireExplicitTarget: true
  plan.messageTool.enabled: true
  plan.messageTool.force: true
  ✅ PASSED

Test 2: Stale webhook caller (sourceReplyDeliveryMode: undefined, message tool disabled)
  owner resolved to: "none"
  plan.sourceReplyDeliveryMode: undefined
  plan.messageTool.enabled: false
  ✅ PASSED

✅ All delivery mode proofs passed
```

### Vitest proof (all 11 tests including executeCronRun-level coverage)

```
$ npx vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > reconstructs a safe delivery plan when sourceDelivery is undefined 53ms
 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > uses resolvedDelivery channel/to for legacy callers without sourceDelivery
 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > reads legacy toolPolicy/sourceReplyDeliveryMode from stale announce caller
 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > reads legacy toolPolicy from stale webhook caller with message tool disabled
 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > passes requireExplicitMessageTarget from legacy toolPolicy in createCronPromptExecutor
 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > still works with a valid sourceDelivery
 ✓ cron > executeCronRun sourceDelivery guard > preserves legacy sourceReplyDeliveryMode: message_tool_only through executeCronRun
 ✓ cron > executeCronRun sourceDelivery guard > defaults to sourceReplyDeliveryMode undefined when legacy mode is absent
 ✓ cron > executeCronRun sourceDelivery guard > passes requireExplicitMessageTarget through executeCronRun fallback
 ✓ cron > executeCronRun sourceDelivery guard > passes requireExplicitMessageTarget=false by default when legacy toolPolicy omits it
 ✓ cron > executeCronRun sourceDelivery guard > reads legacy toolPolicy with message tool disabled through executeCronRun

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
   Duration  837ms
```

### Regression test (existing message-tool-policy tests still pass)

```
$ npx vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  31 passed (31)
   Duration  8.12s
```

### What was not tested
- Live Telegram cron delivery (would require a running Gateway with configured Telegram bot)
- Actual version-skew scenario with stale compiled dist/ artifacts (tested via TypeScript-level params simulation)

Fixes #85051

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()` calls `settleVisibleText()` before TTS synthesis. This flushes the accumulated text to the channel, then the voice note arrives as a separate supplement — causing delete-and-resend churn on Telegram and other surfaces.

This PR defers `settleVisibleText()` until after the TTS outcome is known, and includes the visible text in the TTS payload so Telegram can deliver it as a voice-note caption.

## Root cause

```
Current main flow:
  1. settleVisibleText()     → text message sent to Telegram
  2. maybeApplyTtsToPayload  → voice note synthesized
  3. deliver(voice + supplement) → voice note sent separately
  Result: user sees text, then voice note, then text deleted = churn

Fixed flow:
  1. Skip settleVisibleText()
  2. maybeApplyTtsToPayload  → voice note synthesized
  3. deliver(voice + text as caption) → one combined message
  4. (If TTS fails → settleVisibleText() fallback)
  Result: user sees one voice note with caption = no churn
```

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `dispatch-acp.ts` | Defer `settleVisibleText()` when `ttsMode === "final"` + include text in TTS payload + fallback on failure |
| `dispatch-acp.test.ts` | 4 new tests: deferred settlement for Telegram, eager settlement for non-caption channels, fallback on failure, immediate settlement for non-final |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ auto-reply (54 tests) 30ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  54 passed (54)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `settleVisibleText()` called before TTS synthesis in `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()`, causing text-before-audio churn on Telegram.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `6de206309d`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: Applied PR test file (`dispatch-acp.test.ts` with 54 tests) to unpatched `upstream/main` (`a002c416c7`) source. 1 test fails:

```
 ❯ auto-reply dispatch-acp.test.ts (54 tests | 1 failed) 47ms

 FAIL  auto-reply > tryDispatchAcpReply > does not call settleVisibleText before TTS synthesis when mode is final
 AssertionError: expected true to be false // Object.is equality

 - Expected
 + Received

 - false
 + true

 ❯ dispatch-acp.test.ts:1928:35

 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 53 passed (54)
```

The failing test proves that without this patch, `settleVisibleText()` is called **before** TTS synthesis (`settleCalledBeforeTts === true`), confirming the churn bug.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
git checkout origin/fix/tts-final-text-before-audio-83511
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 ✓ auto-reply dispatch-acp.test.ts (54 tests) 30ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  54 passed (54)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 54 tests pass including the 4 new tests covering: deferred settlement for Telegram (caption-capable channel), eager settlement for non-caption channels (Discord), text fallback when TTS synthesis fails, and immediate settlement when TTS mode is not "final".
- **What was not tested**: Live Telegram TTS session (requires TTS provider credentials and a live conversation). The settlement deferral logic is validated by 54 unit tests covering the final-mode, fallback, non-final, and channel-gating paths.

Closes #83511
